action: Fuse browser render, acoustic loopback, capture-integrity, and muted-control observations into a deny-by-default evidence classification.

progress.finding: Audibility evidence must be conjunctive and fail closed: browser render advance, microphone tone detection, verified capture settings, and a discriminating muted control remain separate observations and only their qualified combination may be labeled strong acoustic evidence.
progress.verification: 5 deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

handoff: Vercel Studio should integrate classifyAcousticEvidence after R-034 and R-035, persist only the derived packet, and require acceptedAsStrongAcousticEvidence before describing a session as microphone-correlated acoustic output. Human audible outcome remains a separate field.
